results.title: The Making of a story: television news
results.creator_credit: Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Compilation tape: 1.”The Making of a Story”. A documentary of how a story is evolved through the process of becoming television for the public. 2. “Television news”. A panel of four, representing professionals from the television networks, news departments as well as researches and academics, give some frameworks for those interested in studying television news. No other credits available.

results.title: Here's to your health: alcohol
results.creator_credit: Craig Reardon, Robert Grant and Victoria. Dept. of Health. Health Promotion Unit
results.brief_description: A program which aims to reduce alcohol consumption by addressing social and cultural aspects which influence teenagers to drink. The program also promotes some alcohol-free activities in the community.

results.title: Challenge of the trade
results.creator_credit: Chris O'Rourke, George Cass and Victoria. Ministry of Education
results.brief_description: Lucianne New follows the career of Dale Baker, principal dancer with the Australian Ballet including footage of Dale performing one of the leading roles in the popular musical “Cats”. Dale shares his thoughts on being male in a world of dance.

results.title: The Australian AIDS tape
results.creator_credit: Nick Torrens, Sabina Wynn, Health Media and Education Centre and New South Wales. Dept. of Health
results.brief_description: Aimed at the general community and provides factual information about AIDS. What HIV/AIDS is. How HIV is transmitted. How HIV is not transmitted. How to prevent it. What to do if you are worried. Offers a telephone number in each State which people can call for further information. A current affair style approach with animation and graphics is used. It was developed for use by educators but can stand alone as an educational resource if no AIDS educator is available. Presented by Geraldine Doogue.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Nick Torrens, Sabina Wynn, Health Media and Education Centre and New South Wales. Dept. of Health

results.title: Ankali means friend
results.brief_description: Ankali is part of Sydney’s Albion Street AIDS Centre. It is run by professional staff and 145 volunteer counsellors. The Ankali project has, since 1985, offered emotional support to people with AIDS. This film focuses on two volunteer counsellors, a psychologist and a teacher, and their clients.

results.title: Shoppingtown
results.creator_credit: David Caesar, Peter Leovic and AFTRS
results.brief_description: Short Australian film by David Caesar which creates a portrait of Parramatta Shoppingtown in August 1986. Initial blank screen isolates people’s words as they voice their aspirations and understanding of happiness. Silence follows as a series of film portraits reveal the inhabitants of Shoppingtown, a shopping centre in suburban Sydney. A rapid montage of images and sounds, most prominently the sounds of the checkout scanner, denote the commercial aspect of a space which has been established in the film as communal and civic. The portraits of shoppers and mall dwellers return, ending in a portrait of the filmmakers themselves.

results.title: Affordable Housing: Dream or Reality
results.brief_description: With changing lifestyles and the enormous cost of buying a freestanding home, this video looks at basic alternative design elements in housing and town planning to accommodate both aesthetically.
